Why care about programming languages July 22, 2026 by Eduardo Bellani

In the age of AI-assisted coding, it’s easy to feel that deep knowledge of programming languages has become obsolete. Why spend years mastering language intricacies when an AI can generate code in any language on demand?

I feel like caring about programming languages and stuff is actually very useless. Am I being too extreme?

I believe this misses something fundamental: a programming language’s worth is derived from the ideas it embodies, not merely its syntax or tooling.

  • on Rust, the borrow checker—reasoning about ownership and lifetimes
  • on Haskell, typeclasses—principled polymorphism and abstraction

These ideas transcend any particular language or tool. They are the conceptual foundation that lets you evaluate generated code and architect systems that remain maintainable as they grow.
